provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These regulations may be cited as the Tanzania Trade development Authority Regulations, 2010 and shall come into operation on a date to be published in the Gazette

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations unless the context otherwise requires: " Act " means the Tanzania Trade Development Authority Act, 2009; [ Act No. 4 of 2009 ] " Authority " means the Tanzania Trade Development Authority as defined under section 3 of the Act ; " Board of Directors " means the Board of Directors established under section 6 of the Act ; " Chairman " means the Chairman as defined under section 3 of the Act ; " Director General " means the Director General as defined under section 3 of the Act ; " Local Government Authority " has the meaning ascribed to it under the Local Government (District Authorities) Act and the Local Government (Urban Authorities) Act ; [Caps 287 and 288] " Members " means a member as defined under section 3 of the Act ; " Minister " means the Minister as defined under section 3 of the Act ; Part II – General regulations

provisions.title: Integration of domestic and foreign markets
provisions.content: (1) The Authority shall, in addition to its functions under section 5(1)(b) of the Act, integrate the domestic market development through— (a) consultation with other stakeholders and advise the government on rules, guidelines and regulations that will guide domestic market operations; (b) developing, monitoring and evaluation of tools for domestic market operations; (c) collection, processing, storage and dissemination of information on domestic market matters; (d) developing and maintaining a database of institutions and stakeholders who are involved in the production of goods and offering services; (e) establishing a market intelligence system to track information; (f) providing technical advice on goods and services based on market requirements; (g) coordinating all local trade fairs and provide technical support; (h) coordinating efforts in the establishment and management of warehousing facilities in local, regional and other overseas markets; (i) advising and coordinating the establishment of facilities and infrastructure to ensure they meet the requirements set by the Authority ; (j) coordinating the participation of Tanzania in international trade fairs and expositions; (k) issuing necessary permits for staging local and international trade fairs; (l) coordinating incoming and outgoing trade missions; (m) coordinating training activities on business management sendees; (n) establishing cooperation between institutions such as Tanzania Investment Centre, Export Processing Zone Authority , Zanzibar Investment Promotion Authority and other relevant trade support or development institutions; (o) consultation with responsible Ministries and relevant institutions to establish Export Development Fund, Entrepreneurship fund and business awards; and (p) fostering better market access for Tanzanian products and services participate in the negotiation process for domestic, regional and international markets. (2) The Authority may require any person, institution or organization connected with trade to furnish information relating to any aspect of trade as per the standard form set out in the first Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Promotion or facilitation of trade fair establishment
provisions.content: The Authority shall: (a) in promoting trade fair facilities: (i) identify the facilities available and their owners: (ii) advise developers to develop the facilities in the required/standards; (b) in facilitating establishment of trade fair facilities: (i) idenify and acquire land; (ii) develop a trade fair master plan; (iii) set the standards and qualities of the facilities; (iv) form partnership with developers in the development of facilities; and (v) manage and maintain facilities.

provisions.title: Establishment and maintenance of trade centers
provisions.content: The Authority shall, in the establishment and maintenance of trade centers— (a) identify and develop the structure; (b) equip the trade centers with market and product information, market intelligence and trade briefs; and (c) management of the trade centers.

provisions.title: Employment of consultants
provisions.content: (1) The Authority shall advice the government or any other institution in the selection and employment of foreign or local trade related consultants and advisers on matters related to trade development. (2) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub-regulation (1) , the Authority shall coordinate the development of foreign trade consultants and advisers of guidelines and terms of reference for selection and employment. (3) A person shall be eligible, on making an application to the Authority , for employment as a foreign trade consultant or adviser if he satisfies the Authority that he— (a) possesses equivalent academic qualification approved by the Authority and has passed the professional interview and written examination; and (b) possesses a minimum of five years working experience in the field. (4) The Government shall, before employing a foreign consultant, utilise the expertise of the local consultants: (5) The Authority shall adhere to the public procurement regulations, wherefore the category of foreign consulting firms and proposal consultation fees as may be provided.

provisions.title: Furnishing of information
provisions.content: Where the Authority requires any person, body institution or organization engaged or connected with trade operations to furnish information, the information sought shall be furnished within 30 days and in the prescribed form set out in the Schedule to these regulations.

provisions.title: Offences and penalties
provisions.content: Any person who contravenes any of the provisions of these Regulations commits an offence and on conviction shall be liable to penalties provided for under the Act .
